page: Amazon Web Services i3en.2xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 8 vCPUs (4 physical cores with hyper-threading) running at 3.1 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. A key characteristic of this instance is its bundled local storage, consisting of 5000 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores falling into the middle 50% tier. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by lower scores in stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded compression tests like gzip and bzip3. Memory bandwidth remains average across both cached small-block and uncached large-block read/write operations. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also demonstrate average performance, while LLM inference tasks show weak to poor performance, particularly during prompt processing on larger models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i3en.2xlarge offers high local storage density and memory capacity relative to its compute resources, making it a qualitative fit for storage-heavy and memory-intensive workloads without the premium of higher-tier compute instances. It is well-suited for database operations, NoSQL databases, distributed file systems, and caching layers that require high-capacity local NVMe storage and moderate memory footprints, rather than compute-bound or heavy mathematical processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services i3en.2xlarge is a storage-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor, featuring an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 8 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory. It is equipped with a substantial 5000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and provides an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows average multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core and cryptographic performance are relatively weak. The instance offers a high ratio of storage and memory to compute resources, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for data-intensive applications. It is best suited for workloads such as transactional databases, Redis caching, and distributed file systems that benefit from high-capacity local NVMe storage.

tagline: A storage-optimized instance featuring high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage and balanced memory for data-intensive database workloads.

64.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
5000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Average performance in database operations and Redis SET benchmarks
Optimized for transactional databases and distributed file systems
